Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff RAYMOND DIGIACOMO, in pro per present (via Zoom videoconference).
Atty. FRANK LA FLEUR, Esq. of the City Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-3826) for Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Carla Short.
Atty. ROSALINE AYOUB, Esq. of Ayoub Law, APC (tel: 818-751-2337) for remaining defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND CARLA SHORT's Motion Of Defendants City And County Of San Francisco And Carla Short To Require Plaintiff To Furnish Security Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 391.1 In The Amount Of $50,000.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified.

The unopposed motion of City & County of San Francisco and defendant Carla Short (collectively City) for an order requiring vexatious litigant Raymond V. DiGiacomo to furnish security pursuant to CCP 391 is denied without prejudice.

The City contends that there is no need for it to prove that plaintiff DiGiacomo is a vexatious litigant in the instant case in light of the court's order in Case No. CGC-20-582151. The City offers CCP 391(b)(4) as its authority. That subdivision states that a vexatious litigant includes a person who "[h]as previously been declared to be a vexatious litigant by any state or federal court of record in any action or proceeding based upon the same or substantially similar facts, transaction, or occurrence." But the City's moving papers make no showing that Case No. CGC-20-582151 is an "action or proceeding based upon the same or substantially similar facts, transaction, or occurrence." Because the City's moving papers do not demonstrate that any of the definitions of "vexatious litigant" under 391(b) apply here, the motion is denied without prejudice.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
